questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as stated in the article?

options.text: To sell Notion templates on a marketplace
options.explanation: Selling templates is mentioned later in a lesson, but it is not the overarching purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To turn curiosity into capability by teaching Notion, building smarter workflows, and offering badges & certifications
options.explanation: This sentence appears at the top of the article and defines the Academy’s mission.

options.text: To provide free consulting services for large enterprises
options.explanation: The article does not mention free consulting; it focuses on learning paths and certification.

options.text: To host live webinars exclusively for advanced users
options.explanation: Webinars are offered, but they are one of many resources, not the primary purpose.

questions.question: Which section of the article explains how a user can become a Notion Certified Admin?

options.explanation: Earning badges is a separate program; certification is described elsewhere.
This heading directly outlines the certification path and its benefits.
Featured lessons list specific courses, not the certification process.
Community involvement is encouraged but does not describe certification.

questions.question: Which lesson is labeled as Intermediate and teaches how to build an intake system using forms, charts, and automations?

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The description explicitly mentions building an intake system with forms, charts, and automations.
Project management focuses on setting up projects and task databases, not intake systems.
Workspace design covers best‑practice design, not intake forms.
Custom Agents automate repeat work but are not described as an intake‑system lesson.

questions.question: How many individual lessons are included in the Advanced Path?

options.explanation: Three lessons correspond to the Essentials Path, not the Advanced Path.
Four lessons describe the Workflows Path.
The article lists "Advanced Path" as containing 5 Lessons.
There is no path with seven lessons mentioned.

questions.question: Which lesson has the longest duration, and how long does it last?

options.explanation: The Templates & marketplace lesson is listed as 40 min, longer than any other lesson.
Workspace design is only 10 min, not the longest.
While 20 min, it is shorter than the 40‑minute Templates lesson.
Custom Agents runs 30 min, still shorter than 40 min.

questions.question: Which path is described as "New to Notion? Gain the basic skills to create, collaborate, and organize your work efficiently"?

options.explanation: The article’s description of the Essentials Path matches that wording.
Workflows Path focuses on creating connected systems with AI, not introductory basics.
Advanced Path targets automation and complex capabilities, not beginners.
Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a path.

questions.question: If a learner wants to understand best practices for designing a Notion workspace, which lesson should they take and what is its difficulty level?

options.explanation: The "Workspace design" lesson is listed as Advanced and covers best‑practice design.
Building basics introduces fundamentals, not advanced workspace design.
Custom Agents focuses on automation, not workspace design.
Project management teaches project & task databases, not overall workspace design.

questions.question: Which path explicitly mentions the use of AI to help create connected systems?

options.explanation: The Workflows Path description states it helps create connected systems "with the help of AI".
Essentials Path targets beginners and does not reference AI.
Advanced Path focuses on automation and architecture but does not specifically call out AI.
The lesson is about creating and selling templates, not AI‑driven systems.
